sutta: monks i will teach you 7 things that cause not decline but 7 so long as the monk shall develop the perception of impermanence of not self of loathsomeness of danger of renunciation of dispassion of cessation growth may be expected not decline but here's another 7 factors then first 1 is perception of impermanence that everything in this world is impermanent and that helps us to have a sense of urgency in life to practice the spiritual path and then and then the next 1 is not self perception of not self in this body and mind to always remind ourselves that this is not i this is not mine this is not myself then the perception of loathsomeness of the body or unattractiveness of the body that is to help us cut less lust for the body then the fourth 1 is danger adinava danger danger in life lessons life is impermanent that any time death may come anytime we may fall sick so we always remember that then the next 1 is pahana renunciation if we are interested in the spiritual path and slowly we slowly let go of our attachments the next 1 is we raga with this passion dispassion with the world as we grow older we don't tend not to get so excited about things in the world as we when we are young we tend to have a lot of passion a lot of excitement about the world and as lonely as we taste all the things in the world and slowly we learn to be dispassionate then cessation decision is the nero ending of every thing everything that arises must cease so it's like a death also so with something we have to remember the nexus
